seasonalGuidance.optimal: May–September (South Spur); winter climbs on north side

june.reason: Peak season; snow stable, weather favorable

july.reason: Very crowded (highway of climbers), soft snow by afternoon

crevasses.timing: Variable; soft snow increases hazard July–August

weather.typical: Exposed ridge; wind, lightning risk, rapid weather changes

crowds.peakTraffic: Highway of climbers on South Spur; peak July weekends

firstAscent.notes: Pre-1980 summit elevation: 9,677 ft. Eruption reduced by ~1,300 ft.

seasonalGuidance.optimal: April–October (Monitor Ridge); winter climbs possible but hazardous

april.reason: Spring snow, firm conditions, lower crowds

may.reason: Best conditions; snow stable, weather favorable

august.reason: Mostly dry, heat; afternoon storms possible

exposure.location: Monitor Ridge has exposure; crater access forbidden (hazard area)
exposure.detail: Post-1980 crater unstable; geothermal hazards; sulfur vents

weather.typical: Exposure on ridge; wind funneling; afternoon thunderstorms Apr–Sep

permits.regulations: Day-use only; no camping above 4,000 ft. Crater access forbidden (geothermal hazard).
